file_name: ADMA BioManufacturing, LLC -  Amendment #3 to Manufacturing Agreement .txt

perturbation.original_text: Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product (the "Minimum Volume") of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD. ADMA accepts and declares that the amount of the liquidated damages is a fair and equitable compensation, and not a penalty, for such failure in reaching the volume commitment within the timelines agreed herein and in regard to the value and use of the Source Plasma. In addition to the Minimum Volume of Product to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount equal to $[***] ([***]) USD for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A, as liquidated damages, and not as a penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum volume of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain damages. In addition to the Minimum Volume of Product to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific amounts for liquidated damages, the text now refers to 'damages' without specifying the amount. This creates an in-text contradiction because later in the same section, it refers to 'an amount for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A' without stating what that amount is, making the enforcement of this clause uncertain. It changes the initially defined liquidated damages to a general form of damages, for which there is no calculation, or amount defined in the legal text. The initial legal text defined this amount, and by changing the definition of the term, it creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to amend the Agreement to impose on ADMA an obligation to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product for that period stalling from Q4 2018 up to Q4 2019, as further specified in Exhibit A attached hereto. Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum volume of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain damages. In addition to the Minimum Volume of Product to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A. The foregoing liquidated damages [***] respect to the [***] within the [***] agreed in this Amendment #3. [***] not be entitled to [***] by this Agreement as a result of [***], including without limitation [***]. Notwithstanding the foregoing, [***], sections 6.1 and 6.2 of the Agreement [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specified liquidated damages amount, replacing it with a general reference to 'damages' and 'an amount.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text because the contradicted text refers to the undefined 'damages' and 'an amount for each Batch of Product' without specifying the monetary value, rendering that portion of the agreement unenforceable and creating a direct conflict regarding the calculation of damages. The change effectively removes a critical definition and makes the agreement internally inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: ADMA agrees to assume the risks of loss to the Sanofi Pasteur Property and shall reimburse Sanofi Pasteur, at the dollar value, the Source Plasma in case of damage or loss.
perturbation.changed_text: ADMA agrees to assume the risks of loss to the Sanofi Pasteur Property in case of damage or loss.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and shall reimburse Sanofi Pasteur, at the dollar value, the Source Plasma', the text creates an ambiguity. It is unclear how Sanofi Pasteur will be made whole in the event of loss or damage to the property. This creates a contradiction with the general premise of risk transfer and insurance. Since there is no requirement for reimbursement. This is an in-text contradiction because the section is about liability, and one would expect that liabilities would be reimbursed to the party that suffered the loss. The edited text, refers to the risk transfer, without specifying what should be done in case of a loss.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At such time that the Source Plasma is delivered to the ADMA loading dock located at 5800 Park of Commerce Blvd NW, Boca Raton, FL 33487, risk of loss for such delivered Source Plasma shall, as between Sanofi Pasteur and ADMA, transfer to ADMA. ADMA agrees to assume the risks of loss to the Sanofi Pasteur Property in case of damage or loss. In consideration of the foregoing, Section 6.5 is deleted in its entirety and replaced by the following: 6.5 Limitation of Liability: In no event shall either party be liable to the other party for incidental, indirect, special and consequential or punitive damages, including without limitation any claims for damages based upon lost profits or lost business opportunity. Except for the obligation of indemnity as set forth in Section 6.1 (c) with respect to claims by third parties for personal injury, illness or death (but not including property damage) resulting from the manufacture of the Product by BPC, aggregate damages for which ADMA shall be liable to Sanofi Pasteur hereunder, including without limitation costs of Source Plasma yield loss and/or rejected Batches, shall not exceed [***]. All claims by Sanofi Pasteur for breach or default under this Agreement shall be brought within [***] year after the cause of action comes into existence or otherwise shall be waived. This limitation of liability will not apply for damages that result from the gross negligence or the willful misconduct of a Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit obligation for ADMA to reimburse Sanofi Pasteur for the dollar value of Source Plasma in case of loss or damage. This omission directly contradicts the intended risk transfer outlined in the 'Contradicted Text,' where ADMA assumes the risk of loss. Without the reimbursement clause, the 'Contradicted Text' section's expectation of ADMA bearing the full risk is undermined, as it's unclear how Sanofi Pasteur would be made whole in case of damage or loss to their property. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because the initial risk transfer clause implied that there would be some form of restitution for a loss, which is no longer explicitly addressed. The absence of a reimbursement clause makes it incompatible with the intention of risk transfer described in the contradicted text, creating uncertainty and a potential legal issue about the extent of ADMA's liability.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties further agree to amend that certain Quality and Technical Agreement between Sanofi Pasteur and ADMA (as successor to BPC) effective as of September 15, 2015 (the "Quality Agreement") to modify the address where the Source Plasma shall be stocked and the conditions associated therewith, as well as to ensure consistency with the other terms of this Amendment. The Parties shall use best efforts to complete such amendment to the Quality Agreement within 60 days after the Amendment Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties further agree to amend that certain Quality and Technical Agreement between Sanofi Pasteur and ADMA (as successor to BPC) effective as of September 15, 2015 to modify the address where the Source Plasma shall be stocked and the conditions associated therewith. The Parties shall use best efforts to complete such amendment to the Quality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'as well as to ensure consistency with the other terms of this Amendment' and 'within 60 days after the Amendment Effective Date', the parties are required to make an effort towards the compliance of the terms without any type of consistenty and there is no longer a timeframe for it, resulting in it becoming an open and vague definition. Due to this change, the text contradicts itself because it doesn't define consistency terms, or when the Quality Agreement should be complete.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties further agree to amend that certain Quality and Technical Agreement between Sanofi Pasteur and ADMA (as successor to BPC) effective as of September 15, 2015 to modify the address where the Source Plasma shall be stocked and the conditions associated therewith. The Parties shall use best efforts to complete such amendment to the Quality Agreement. 9. All other terms of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect except to the extent superseded by the terms of this Amendment #3. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereby have caused this Amendment #3 to the Agreement to be executed and the persons signing below warrant that they are duly authorized to sign for and on behalf of their respective Parties. Made in two original copies.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains a clause specifying that the amendment to the Quality Agreement should ensure consistency with other terms of the amendment and be completed within 60 days. The changed text removes both the consistency requirement and the 60-day timeframe. The contradicted text relies on the understanding that the Quality Agreement amendment would be consistent with other terms and implicitly expects a defined timeframe for completion. By removing these elements, the changed text introduces ambiguity and a lack of clarity regarding the expected standards for the Quality Agreement, thereby creating a contradiction with the contradicted text's assumption of consistency and a defined timeframe.
